reviews.review_msg: Michele is extremely sweet and gives great feedback. Her lectures get a little dry sometimes, but she is always trying to make jokes around class. There are assignments every week for the final portfolio, so as long as you're on top of that, you will do fine.

Michele was actually a really great prof! She's caring, her office hours and very helpful and make sure to bring your assignments to her to read over. However, she is a tough grader, she doesn't give out anything above a B+ easily, so you must work hard in this course to get the grade you want.

Read your theory from the text and read your feedback on your assignments.  Gives you a great framework of theory to apply to your assignments.  Michele is also very postitive, dynamic, has personality galore and cloaks her intellectual abilities with a nuturing teaching style.

You don't take this class for an easy A. You really have to work for it, BUT it's totally worth it. You will learn things that will really help you in your undergrad career. She's also very understanding about deadlines and such. Very approachable.
Very nice and approachable professor,  but she is an extremely hard marker. Make use of office hours as she will proof read and edit your papers for you!

It's a hard class, specially if you're taking it straight out of highschool. Also in the english department they very rarely give out A+ so its a toughie. Prof was very helpful and liked to engage students - the seminar format is really great - and make sure you go to office hours and make any adjustmentscorrections that she suggests.

Overall she is a decent prof who tries to engage students and is very into her teaching as well. However, she is a tough marker and don't always provide help even when it is promised (my half-hour appointment was made into 5 and no extra office hour was provided). Show up to class and read and follow the textbook. Long lectures.

She is an extremely hard marker, don't expect an A in her class, if you can scrap by with a B, consider yourself lucky. You forfeit your participation grade if you skip class twice. Giltrow worshipper, she has an unhealthy obsession with her, my biggest mistake was taking this class in my first year do not take it unless ABSOLUTELY necessary.

The TM is in charge of the online class. This class is quite hard I managed to get a B. But this class is no walk in the park. It's basically all assignments (writing) and 1 midterm. The marking for essays is like 4th year marking. So pretty difficult.

I never liked high school writing. I reluctantly took this course because my second degree required it. There was a lot of readings but they were useful. I liked this course because its explanations made sense. My strong vocabulary helps me in writing "abstractions" -- may be a student should take this in his final year? Or may be 1st year? Hmm...
